title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: In extreme cases, this disease can lead to a heart attack or a stroke.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: These factors for atherosclerosis include high blood pressure, high cholesterol, tobacco smoke, diabetes, obesity, and physical inactivity.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Although the exact causes of atherosclerosis are not clear, many scientists think it begins with damage to the inner wall of an artery, called the endothelium.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Over time, substances traveling in the blood, such as cholesterol, fats, and cellular waste products accumulate inside the damaged area of the arterial wall. Chemical reactions occurring within the build-up of material cause cholesterol molecules to This initiates an inflammatory response in which the endothelial cells, at the damage site, release chemicals that signal a call for help.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: In response, monocytes from the bloodstream travel to the damage site. Demulation from oxidized cholesterol converts the monocytes into macrophages.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: The macrophages eat and digest the cholesterol molecules. As a result of this process, the macrophages change into foam cells, which accumulate to form plaque.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: As the plaque increases in size, the arterial wall thickens and hardens. At the same time, smooth muscle cells within the arterial wall begin to multiply. Most of the smooth muscle cells move to the surface of the plaque. These cells contribute to the formation of a firm, fibrous cap covering the plaque.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Eventually, the passageway through the artery narrows, enough to reduce blood flow and the amount of oxygen received by the organs it supplies.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Over time, the cap may erode and break open, releasing plaque into the bloodstream. The plaque can flow downstream and contribute to the formation of the blood clot, which can stop blood flow.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: As a result, limited blood supply is available to the areas surrounding the partially blocked artery, degrading and potentially killing the neighboring tissue. Significant damage in organs, such as the heart or brain, can result in a heart attack or a stroke.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into the artery and advance a balloon-tipped catheter over this wire and into position at the side of the blockage.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Rapid inflation and deflation of the small balloon pushes the vessel wall out, reestablishing blood flow through the artery. You may feel some chest pain while this is happening.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: The same procedure is repeated for each blockage being treated.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Afterwards, your doctor will withdraw the deflated balloon and catheter from your arteries.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Some patients will receive a coronary stent, which is a small tube made of wire mesh, designed to prop open the artery once it has been treated. A collapsed stent is placed over the deflated balloon as it's moved into position.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: When the balloon is inflated, the stent expands and locks into place.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: After the balloon and catheter are withdrawn, the stent remains behind to hold the artery open permanently.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft, or cabbage, is performed to improve circulation to the heart muscle in people with severe coronary artery disease. In this procedure, a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected or grafted to the blocked coronary artery. A grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen rich blood to the heart muscle. One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin over your breastbone or sternum. He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system will be connected to a cardiopulmonary bypass pump or heart lung machine. This machine temporarily performs the functions of your heart and lungs during the surgery, allowing your heart to be stopped while the surgeon soars the grafts into place.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: One of two blood vessels is typically used for the grafts, the internal thoracic artery, in the chest or the saffinous vein in the leg.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery just beyond the blockage. Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: For the saffinous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta and the other end to the narrowed artery just beyond the blockage.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon will use electrical signals to restore the heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart. Once your heart is again beating normally, the heart lung machine will be disconnected.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed. A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.
